message:
chore(beads): close 4 more beads resolved by matrix STRICT passes (w579, 1pe7, zk9q, 2di1)

All four were left open despite their underlying live-oracle tests passing 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1:

- w579: CLIENT INFO/ID/HELLO id canonicalizers + SETINFO/NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH paths — core_client 100%
- 1pe7: DEBUG OBJECT/DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE canonicalizers + SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE fix — core_debug 100%
- zk9q: HGETALL field iteration order canonicalizer — core_hash 100%
- 2di1: CONFIG SET/GET/REWRITE wording + defaults — core_config 100%

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-1gyg","title":"redis parity: LATENCY subcommands should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY/RESET/GRAPH/DOCTOR/HISTOGRAM as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/latency-latest.json, latency-history.json, latency-reset.json, latency-graph.json, latency-doctor.json, and latency-histogram.json. The shared latency_cmd implementation at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13086 has no script_nesting_level guard for any subcommand. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT handling and regression coverage across the subcommand matrix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:23.464539739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:21:24.238343081Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:21:24.236072353Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-1ng7","title":"Implement command propagation from primary to connected replicas","description":"# Command Propagation to Replicas

## What
When FrankenRedis is acting as a primary and replicas are connected, write commands must be propagated to all connected replicas in real-time.

## Current State
The fr-server has `replication_follow_up_bytes()` which serializes commands for replication. Replica connections are tracked. The gap is that write commands are not actually pushed to replica write buffers after execution, and the backlog buffer is not populated.

## Implementation
1. After each write command execution in the main event loop, check if there are connected replicas
2. Serialize the command as RESP (already done by replication_follow_up_bytes)
3. Push the serialized bytes into each replica connection write_buf AND the backlog buffer
4. Advance replication offset by the number of bytes pushed
5. Handle slow replicas: if write_buf exceeds output_buffer_limit, disconnect replica with warning
6. Ensure read-only commands (GET, KEYS, etc.) are NOT propagated

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: Write command increments replication offset
- Test 2: Read command does NOT increment replication offset
- Test 3: Offset advances by exact RESP-serialized byte count
- Test 4: MULTI/EXEC propagates all commands in the transaction

## Integration Tests
- Test 5: Start primary, connect replica, SET 1000 keys on primary, verify all 1000 appear on replica
- Test 6: Slow replica (inject delay) — verify primary disconnects it after buffer overflow
- Test 7: Multiple replicas — verify all receive the same commands
- Test 8: INFO replication on primary shows connected_slaves count

## Logging
- Log propagation: "REPL: propagating SET key (42 bytes) to 2 replicas"
- Log offset advancement
- Log slow replica disconnection with reason

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need a connection to propagate to
- Backlog buffer (iz1j) — propagated bytes go here for partial resync
- RDB snapshot streaming (de0t) — replica must have initial state before streaming

## Files
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (propagation after command dispatch)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (offset tracking)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.983808258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898911059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898680643Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: propagate_writes_to_replicas() at main.rs:2255 pushes AOF-serialized writes to all connected replica write buffers. Called in main event loop at line 534 after every command batch. Unit test at line 3564 verifies the flow.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:44.677262603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-de0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:14:57.688577941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.790873067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1qdg","title":"Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands","description":"# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1v8b","title":"Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake","description":"# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-29c7","title":"Add RESP parser fuzz test for crash resilience on malformed input","description":"# RESP Parser Fuzz Test

## What
A property-based test that feeds random/malformed bytes to the RESP parser and verifies it never panics, only returns Ok or Err.

## Why
The RESP parser is the first code that touches untrusted network input. A panic crashes the entire server. Property-based testing (proptest) is the practical approach since cargo-fuzz requires nightly and special setup.

## Implementation
1. Add proptest as a dev-dependency to fr-protocol
2. New test in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs test module:
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn parse_frame_never_panics(data: Vec<u8>) {
           let _ = parse_frame(&data); // Ok or Err, never panic
       }
   }
   ```
3. Also test parse_frame_with_config with various ParserConfig settings
4. Also test with structured semi-valid input: valid RESP prefix byte + random payload
5. Run for at least 10,000 cases (proptest default is 256, increase with PROPTEST_CASES env)
6. Fix any panics found

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-protocol -- parse_frame_never_panics` passes
- Run with PROPTEST_CASES=100000 in CI

## Files
- crates/fr-protocol/Cargo.toml (add proptest dev-dependency)
- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (add proptest tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:50.074946085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736277717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736003488Z","close_reason":"Added 4 proptest fuzz tests to fr-protocol: parse_frame_never_panics, parse_frame_with_config_never_panics, parse_frame_with_tight_limits_never_panics, parse_frame_with_resp_prefix_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random input cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-2auy","title":"Add minimal client session context to Store for CLIENT stubs in dispatch_argv","description":"# Add minimal client context to Store for CLIENT command in dispatch_argv

## Problem
CLIENT ID/GETNAME/SETNAME/LIST/INFO in fr-command return hardcoded values (id=1, name=nil, etc.) because they don't have access to the ClientSession. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") get 1 instead of their real client ID.

## Design
Add a \"current client context\" to Store that the runtime populates before dispatching:
```rust
pub struct DispatchClientContext {
    pub client_id: u64,
    pub client_name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    pub db_index: usize,
    pub flags: String,   // e.g., \"N\" for normal
    pub peer_addr: String,
}
```

The runtime already swaps sessions before dispatch — it can populate this context at the same time.

## Implementation
1. Add pub dispatch_client_ctx: DispatchClientContext to Store
2. In Runtime::execute_frame (before dispatch_argv call), populate store.dispatch_client_ctx from current session
3. Update fr-command's client_cmd to read from store.dispatch_client_ctx:
   - CLIENT ID: return ctx.client_id
   - CLIENT GETNAME: return ctx.client_name
   - CLIENT SETNAME: update ctx.client_name (runtime will read it back)
   - CLIENT LIST/INFO: format using ctx fields
4. After dispatch_argv returns, runtime reads back any changes (e.g., SETNAME)

## Scope limitation
CLIENT LIST in Redis returns ALL connected clients. Through dispatch_argv, we can only report the current client. This is acceptable for Lua scripts (they typically only need their own info). The runtime's CLIENT LIST already returns all clients.

## Depends on
- No hard dependencies, but nice to land after the counter foundation

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") and gets a real ID (not 1)
- Unit test: populate context, verify CLIENT GETNAME returns it

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (DispatchClientContext struct, Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (client_cmd reads context)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (populate context before dispatch)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:41.559944609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359816290Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359474203Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-2di1","title":"[HIGH] CONFIG GET/SET/REWRITE parity: port/dir/maxmemory defaults, multi-pattern order, case-insensitive echo, upstream error wording","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime (29 divergences): (a) CONFIG GET returns ephemeral values (port, dir) that differ between our fixture-pinned defaults and the upstream test-spawned instance; needs comparator ignore-list or harness-derived expected values. (b) CONFIG GET on multi-pattern invocations returns keys in different order — add comparator canonicalization like HGETALL. (c) CONFIG GET is case-insensitive on the reply key name — upstream returns the requested casing verbatim. (d) CONFIG SET unknown-parameter / non-integer / negative-integer / policy-invalid error wording all differ from upstream's 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument ...)' template. (e) CONFIG REWRITE returns OK where upstream returns 'ERR The server is running without a config file'. (f) hash-max-listpack-entries default 128 vs upstream 512; proto-max-bulk-len default 512000000 vs upstream 536870912. (g) active-defrag-enabled accepted silently where upstream rejects (not supported). (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization: ours echoes as-written, upstream normalizes to AKE order. Fix in fr-command CONFIG SET/GET error wording + fr-config defaults + harness comparator.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T08:45:44.648576531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:22:59.147705277Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-w1m","title":"Wire CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold (lua-time-limit) to runtime","description":"## Problem

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line 1294 defines `const COMMAND_TIME_BUDGET_MS: u128 = 5000`. This matches Redis's busy-reply-threshold default (5000ms) but is not configurable.

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold <milliseconds> controls when Redis starts rejecting commands with BUSY errors during long-running scripts or operations. CONFIG SET lua-time-limit is the legacy name.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add command_time_budget_ms to ServerState (initialized to 5000)
2. Handle CONFIG SET busy-reply-threshold and lua-time-limit (both set the same value)
3. Replace the const usage with the runtime field

 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
